column1: # HOW TO USE - Market Prices (now auto-updating)
# Market <-> ledger link: the Holdings tab values every team's shares by VLOOKUP-ing the Current Price (col B) by name. Col B is now a FORMULA, not typed.

# AUTO-UPDATE: the round-driver cell is R1 (the number just right of 'Current Round →', range 1-13). At each round CLOSE, change ONLY R1 to that round number - all 9 Current Prices (B2:B10) jump to that round's closing price via =INDEX($C:$O,1,$R$1), and Holdings revalue automatically. No more typing 9 prices.
# Columns C-O are the official closing prices per round (R1..R13), taken straight from dashboard/market-script.json (anchors index 1..13). They are fixed - do not edit during play.

# If the Current Price cells show the text '=INDEX(...)' after import, your import didn't convert formulas: retype the '=' in B2 and drag down to B10, or re-import with 'Convert text to numbers/formulas' enabled.

# Stock prices move on a $10 grid (Rule of Tens - stocks only). Keep the 9 instrument names in column A spelled EXACTLY as shown (VLOOKUP keys) - do not rename or reorder. The names are real-world examples (S&P 500, Maybank, Tesla, Dogecoin...) so participants learn what kind of asset each is.

# DIVIDENDS (col P, Div/share): a FIXED dollar dividend paid per share, every payday. Only the 4 stable stocks pay (99 Speedmart/Tenaga/Maybank/Maxis = $5); the S&P 500, growth stocks, and Dogecoin = $0. It's a flat constant (same all 13 rounds) - the yield floats because price changes, not the $5. Holdings col M reads this column (P2:P10) to auto-compute each team's monthly dividend; see Holdings + Financial Statement setup.
